revision.comment: 'constructive'
revision.text:
{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Amenonuhoko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Authority control}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Mythological islands]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]]

Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Authority control}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Mythological islands]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]]

{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Amenonuhoko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Authority control}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Mythological islands]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[en:Onogoro Island]]

{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Amenonuhoko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Authority control}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Mythological islands]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[category:dd]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[category:dd]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[en:Onogoro Island]]

In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.

revision.text:
{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Amenonuhoko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Authority control}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Mythological islands]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia21]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia30]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia21]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Islands of Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia30]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia30]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia30]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia30]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia30]] [[en:Onogoro Island]]

Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Authority control}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Mythological islands]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Islands of Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Onogoro Island]]

{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Amenonuhoko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Authority control}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Mythological islands]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Islands of Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Onogoro Island]]

Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Authority control}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Mythological islands]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Islands of Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:Images]] [[en:Onogoro Island]]

revision.text:
{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Amenonuhoko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Authority control}} {{Jmyth navbox long}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Mythological islands]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Islands of Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Images]] [[en:Onogoro Island]]

The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.

{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Amenonuhoko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.

This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.

revision.text:
{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Amenonuhoko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.

{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Amenonuhoko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.

{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Amenonuhoko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.

revision.text:
{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Amenonuhoko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.

{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Amenonuhoko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, {{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Amenonuhoko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} revision.text:
{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Amenonuhoko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Amenonuhoko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Mythological islands]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] [[Category:Onogoro Island]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Onogoro Island]] [[sn:Onogoro Island]]

{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.

Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Mythological islands]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] [[Category:Onogoro Island]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Onogoro Island]] [[sn:Onogoro Island]] [[Category:New]]

{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Ame-no-Nuboko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] [[Category:Onogoro Island]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Onogoro Island]] [[sn:Onogoro Island]]

{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Ame-no-Nuboko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[Hiruko]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[:en:Awajishima|Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] }} [[en:Onogoro Island]] [[sn:Onogoro Island]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] [[ca:Onogoro]] [[es:Onogoro]] [[fr:Onogoro]] [[id:Pulau Onogoro]] [[ja:オノゴロ島]] [[ko:오노고로섬]] [[pl:Onogoro-jima]] [[ru:Оногоро]]

On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.

{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[:en:Island|Island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Ame-no-Nuboko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[:en:Column|Column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu (mythology)]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[:en:Awajishima|Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
