revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[:en:Island|Island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[:en:Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Ame-no-Nuboko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[palace]] on top with a great [[:en:Column|Column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu (mythology)]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[:en:Awajishima|Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] }} [[en:Onogoro Island]] [[sn:Onogoro Island]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ca:Onogoro]] [[es:Onogoro]] [[fr:Onogoro]] [[id:Pulau Onogoro]] [[ja:オノゴロ島]] [[ko:오노고로섬]] [[pl:Onogoro-jima]] [[ru:Оногоро]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2985158]] Wikidata name: Onogoro Island [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[:en:Island|Island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[:en:Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Ame-no-Nuboko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[:en:Palace|Palace]] on top with a great [[:en:Column|Column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu (mythology)]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[:en:Awajishima|Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] }} [[en:Onogoro Island]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ca:Onogoro]] [[es:Onogoro]] [[fr:Onogoro]] [[id:Pulau Onogoro]] [[ja:オノゴロ島]] [[ko:오노고로섬]] [[pl:Onogoro-jima]] [[ru:Оногоро]] {{wikidata link|Q2985158}}

The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[:en:Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Ame-no-Nuboko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[:en:Palace|Palace]] on top with a great [[:en:Column|Column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu (mythology)]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[:en:Awajishima|Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] }} [[en:Onogoro Island]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ca:Onogoro]] [[es:Onogoro]] [[fr:Onogoro]] [[id:Pulau Onogoro]] [[ja:オノゴロ島]] [[ko:오노고로섬]] [[pl:Onogoro-jima]] [[ru:Оногоро]] {{wikidata link|Q2985158}}

[[ca:Onogoro]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[es:Onogoro]][[fr:Onogoro]][[id:Pulau Onogoro]][[ja:オノゴロ島]][[ko:오노고로섬]][[pl:Onogoro-jima]][[ru:Оногоро]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2985158]] Wikidata name: Onogoro Island [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[:en:Island|Island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[:en:Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Ame-no-Nuboko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[:en:Palace|Palace]] on top with a great [[:en:Column|Column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu (mythology)]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[:en:Awajishima|Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] }} [[en:Onogoro Island]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ca:Onogoro]] [[es:Onogoro]] [[fr:Onogoro]] [[id:Pulau Onogoro]] [[ja:オノゴロ島]] [[ko:오노고로섬]] [[pl:Onogoro-jima]] [[ru:Оногоро]] {{wikidata link|Q2985158}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

They built a [[:en:Palace|Palace]] on top with a great [[:en:Column|Column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu (mythology)]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[:en:Awajishima|Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.

The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[:en:Awajishima|Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima]]). In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] }} [[en:Onogoro Island]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ca:Onogoro]] [[es:Onogoro]] [[fr:Onogoro]] [[id:Pulau Onogoro]] [[ja:オノゴロ島]] [[ko:오노고로섬]] [[pl:Onogoro-jima]] [[ru:Оногоро]] {{wikidata link|Q2985158}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ca:Onogoro]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[es:Onogoro]][[fr:Onogoro]][[id:Pulau Onogoro]][[ja:オノゴロ島]][[ko:오노고로섬]][[pl:Onogoro-jima]][[ru:Оногоро]] [[ca:Onogoro]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[es:Onogoro]][[fr:Onogoro]][[id:Pulau Onogoro]][[ja:オノゴロ島]][[ko:오노고로섬]][[pl:Onogoro-jima]][[ru:Оногоро]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2985158]] Wikidata name: Onogoro Island [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[:en:Island|Island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[:en:Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Ame-no-Nuboko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[:en:Palace|Palace]] on top with a great [[:en:Column|Column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu (mythology)]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[:en:Awajishima|Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.

Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Onogoro Island]] [[ca:Onogoro]] [[es:Onogoro]] [[fr:Onogoro]] [[id:Pulau Onogoro]] [[ja:オノゴロ島]] [[ko:오노고로섬]] [[pl:Onogoro-jima]] [[ru:Оногоро]] {{wikidata link|Q2985158}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
[[ca:Onogoro]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[es:Onogoro]][[fr:Onogoro]][[id:Pulau Onogoro]][[ja:オノゴロ島]][[ko:오노고로섬]][[pl:Onogoro-jima]][[ru:Оногоро]] [[ca:Onogoro]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[es:Onogoro]][[fr:Onogoro]][[id:Pulau Onogoro]][[ja:オノゴロ島]][[ko:오노고로섬]][[pl:Onogoro-jima]][[ru:Оногоро]] [[ca:Onogoro]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[es:Onogoro]][[fr:Onogoro]][[id:Pulau Onogoro]][[ja:オノゴロ島]][[ko:오노고로섬]][[pl:Onogoro-jima]][[ru:Оногоро]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2985158]] Wikidata name: Onogoro Island [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important [[:en:Island|Island]] in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''[[:en:Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten]]'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] churned the sea with the [[Ame-no-Nuboko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a [[:en:Palace|Palace]] on top with a great [[:en:Column|Column]] in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where [[:en:Ebisu (mythology)|Ebisu (mythology)]] (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kojiki-den|Kojiki-den]]}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|[[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]]}} scholar [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[:en:Awajishima|Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or [[:en:Tomogashima|Tomogashima]]). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ([[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ([[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Onogoro Island]] [[ca:Onogoro]] [[es:Onogoro]] [[fr:Onogoro]] [[id:Pulau Onogoro]] [[ja:オノゴロ島]] [[ko:오노고로섬]] [[pl:Onogoro-jima]] [[ru:Оногоро]] {{wikidata link|Q2985158}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:Wikidata has English label]]" to "[[Category:qq]]"
revision.text:
[[ca:Onogoro]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[es:Onogoro]][[fr:Onogoro]][[id:Pulau Onogoro]][[ja:オノゴロ島]][[ko:오노고로섬]][[pl:Onogoro-jima]][[ru:Оногоро]] [[ca:Onogoro]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[es:Onogoro]][[fr:Onogoro]][[id:Pulau Onogoro]][[ja:オノゴロ島]][[ko:오노고로섬]][[pl:Onogoro-jima]][[ru:Оногоро]] [[ca:Onogoro]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[es:Onogoro]][[fr:Onogoro]][[id:Pulau Onogoro]][[ja:オノゴロ島]][[ko:오노고로섬]][[pl:Onogoro-jima]][[ru:Оногоро]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2985158]] Wikidata name: Onogoro Island [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important {{ill|Island|en|Island|WD=Q23442|lt=Island}} in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''{{ill|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|en|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|WD=Q4093013|lt=Nihon Kokugo Daijiten}}'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} churned the sea with the [[Ame-no-Nuboko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a {{ill|Palace|en|Palace|WD=Q16560|lt=Palace}} on top with a great {{ill|Column|en|Column|WD=Q4817|lt=Column}} in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu (mythology)}} (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|{{ill|Kojiki-den|en|Kojiki-den|WD=Q11410835|lt=Kojiki-den}}}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|{{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku}}}} scholar {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga}} asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[:en:Awajishima|Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or {{ill|Tomogashima|en|Tomogashima|WD=Q7820112|lt=Tomogashima}}). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ({{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|WD=Q5287|lt=Japanese}}: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ({{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|WD=Q5287|lt=Japanese}}: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Jmyth navbox long}} [[en:Onogoro Island]] [[ca:Onogoro]] [[es:Onogoro]] [[fr:Onogoro]] [[id:Pulau Onogoro]] [[ja:オノゴロ島]] [[ko:오노고로섬]] [[pl:Onogoro-jima]] [[ru:Оногоро]] {{wikidata link|Q2985158}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu (mythology)}} (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|{{ill|Kojiki-den|en|Kojiki-den|WD=Q11410835|lt=Kojiki-den}}}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|{{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku}}}} scholar {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga}} asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[:en:Awajishima|Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or {{ill|Tomogashima|en|Tomogashima|WD=Q7820112|lt=Tomogashima}}). revision.text:
[[ca:Onogoro]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[es:Onogoro]][[fr:Onogoro]][[id:Pulau Onogoro]][[ja:オノゴロ島]][[ko:오노고로섬]][[pl:Onogoro-jima]][[ru:Оногоро]] [[ca:Onogoro]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[es:Onogoro]][[fr:Onogoro]][[id:Pulau Onogoro]][[ja:オノゴロ島]][[ko:오노고로섬]][[pl:Onogoro-jima]][[ru:Оногоро]] [[ca:Onogoro]][[en:Onogoro Island]][[es:Onogoro]][[fr:Onogoro]][[id:Pulau Onogoro]][[ja:オノゴロ島]][[ko:오노고로섬]][[pl:Onogoro-jima]][[ru:Оногоро]] {{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important {{ill|Island|en|Island|WD=Q23442|lt=Island}} in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''{{ill|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|en|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|WD=Q4093013|lt=Nihon Kokugo Daijiten}}'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} churned the sea with the [[Ame-no-Nuboko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a {{ill|Palace|en|Palace|WD=Q16560|lt=Palace}} on top with a great {{ill|Column|en|Column|WD=Q4817|lt=Column}} in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu (mythology)}} (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|{{ill|Kojiki-den|en|Kojiki-den|WD=Q11410835|lt=Kojiki-den}}}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|{{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku}}}} scholar {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga}} asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[:en:Awajishima|Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or {{ill|Tomogashima|en|Tomogashima|WD=Q7820112|lt=Tomogashima}}). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ({{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|WD=Q5287|lt=Japanese}}: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ({{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|WD=Q5287|lt=Japanese}}: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Jmyth navbox long}} [[en:Onogoro Island]] [[ca:Onogoro]] [[es:Onogoro]] [[fr:Onogoro]] [[id:Pulau Onogoro]] [[ja:オノゴロ島]] [[ko:오노고로섬]] [[pl:Onogoro-jima]] [[ru:Оногоро]] {{wikidata link|Q2985158}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

{{short description|Island in Japanese mythology}} '''Onogoroshima''' ({{Lang|ja-latn|[[Kojiki]]}}: {{lang|ja|淤能碁呂島}} ''Onogoro-shima''; {{Lang|ja-latn|[[Nihon Shoki]]}}: {{lang|ja|磤馭慮島}} ''Onogoro-jima'') is an important {{ill|Island|en|Island|WD=Q23442|lt=Island|1=Island}} in [[Shinto]]. The name derives from {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:自#Japanese|自]]}} (''ono'', "self") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:凝る|凝る]]}} (''koru'', "to coalesce, to aggregate together") + {{lang|ja|[[:wikt:島#Japanese|島]]}} (''shima'', "island").''{{ill|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|en|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|WD=Q4093013|lt=Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|1=Nihon Kokugo Daijiten}}'', entry available online [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%A3%A4%E9%A6%AD%E7%9B%A7%E5%B3%B6-2017339 here] (in Japanese) ==Shinto account== According to the [[Kojiki]], Onogoroshima was created (''[[kuniumi]]'') when the divine couple [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} churned the sea with the [[Ame-no-Nuboko]] (heavenly spear) from their vantage point on the floating bridge of heaven.Kurano, {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, page 53 When they raised the spear from the ocean, drops fell from the spear, forming the island. They built a {{ill|Palace|en|Palace|WD=Q16560|lt=Palace|1=Palace}} on top with a great {{ill|Column|en|Column|WD=Q4817|lt=Column|1=Column}} in the middle. The siblings walked around this pillar in opposite directions, and when they encountered each other, they were married. This island is where {{ill|Ebisu (mythology)|en|Ebisu (mythology)|WD=Q1129330|lt=Ebisu (mythology)|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} (Ebisu) was born. The island is mentioned only three times in the [[Kojiki]], at ch.3:3, ch:7:25, and ch.111:32.Philippi's translation, p. 49, p. 57, and p. 306. ==Real-world placement== In his {{Lang|ja-latn|{{ill|Kojiki-den|en|Kojiki-den|WD=Q11410835|lt=Kojiki-den|1=Kojiki-den}}}}, a commentary on the {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, the great {{Lang|ja-latn|{{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku|1=Kokugaku}}}} scholar {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga|1=Motoori Norinaga}} asserted that it referred to one of the small islands near [[:en:Awajishima|Awajishima]] (e.g. [[Nushima]] or {{ill|Tomogashima|en|Tomogashima|WD=Q7820112|lt=Tomogashima|1=Tomogashima}}). On the island of Nu/ Nushima ({{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|WD=Q5287|lt=Japanese|1=Japanese language}}: 沼島), there is a shrine commemorating both Izanagi and Izanami, Onogoro-jinja ({{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|WD=Q5287|lt=Japanese|1=Japanese language}}: 自凝神社) on top of a sacred hill revered by all Japanese is where the first island of Japan is created – i.e. Nushima, then followed by other creation of islands. == Folklore of the area surrounding of Onogoro Island == ; Nushima (沼島) : Nushima is a mysteriously shaped island like a ''[[magatama]]'' from the sky, located 4.6km above the southern sea of Awaji Island. In 1994, a very rare rock was discovered on this island, and it is called "''Sayagata-shūkyoku'' (鞘型褶曲, i.e. "Sheath-shaped Fold)", which is regarded as the "''Chikyū-no-Shiwa'' (地球のしわ, i.e. "Wrinkle of the Earth")". It is around 100 million years old. : [[File:Kamitategamiiwa.jpg|thumb|Kamitategami-Iwa, in the island of Nushima]]There are many rocks and reefs on the shoreline of Numashima, and on the southeast coast, a towering giant rock “''Kamitategami-Iwa'' (上立神岩)” with a height of about 30 metres which looks like a spear, has become a reminiscent symbol and icon that became the origin of the "Birth of the Japanese nation" (''[[kamiumi]]''). This rock, which appears as in mythology, is also called "''Ame-no-mihashira'' (天の御柱, "the Pillar of Heaven") and is said to be the one Izanagi and Izanami descended to.
==Notes== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} {{reflist}} ==References== *Donald L. Philippi tr. {{Lang|ja-latn|Kojiki}}, Princeton University Press, Tokyo University Press, 1969 * {{cite book | last = Kurano | first = Kenji | authorlink = |author2=Yūkichi Takeda | title = Kojiki, Norito | series = Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei | publisher = [[Iwanami Shoten Publishing|Iwanami Shoten]] | year = 1958 | location = [[Tokyo|Tōkyō]] | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-00-060001-X }} {{Jmyth navbox long}} {{wikidata link|Q2985158|ca|Onogoro|en|Onogoro Island|es|Onogoro|fr|Onogoro|id|Pulau Onogoro|ja|オノゴロ島|ko|오노고로섬|pl|Onogoro-jima|ru|Оногоро}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Islands of Japan]] [[Category:Locations in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
